troubleshooting.title: Portfolio Manager Does Not Launch
troubleshooting.shortdesc: The application fails to open, exits immediately, or shows no window after launching from the Dock or a shell script.

condition.p: You launch Portfolio Manager from the Dock icon or by running bash launch.sh and one of the following occurs:

ul.li: A window flashes briefly and closes.
The terminal shows a Python traceback.

step.cmd: If the version is below 3.11, download and install Python 3.11 or later from python.org.
Delete the existing virtual environment and relaunch:

p.b: Tkinter is not installed or not linked correctly.

info.p: If a small test window appears, Tkinter is working. If the command fails, proceed to the next step.

p.b: A dependency is missing or the virtual environment is corrupted.

step.cmd: Delete the virtual environment and reinstall dependencies:

info.p: The launch script recreates the virtual environment and installs all dependencies.

p.b: A database migration error occurred during startup.

step.cmd: Check the application log for migration errors:
If a migration error is shown, see .
